description: Bird flushed from about 30 feet in front of me. It flew low, with moderately long glides, and downward flaps, wings held above horizontal during glides. Base color of body below was brown. The underparts were barred with darker brown. Belly/vent as brown as chest. Wings appeared short and distinctly rounded when compared with those of Common Nighthawk. The white bar on the wing was placed more than 1/2, but probably not quite 2/3, of distance from "wrist" to tip of wing; it seemed to fully reach leading edge of wing (ie, outer web of outer primary). Chin did not have bright white mark(s) that I associate with adult males of either Lesser or Common Nighthawk. I did not have time to study tail well and was unable to discern spotting pattern on primaries. The wings, however, were not in molt.
